issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://map.naver.com/p?c=15.00,0,0,0,adh&p=G8MCOE5tzwznzZPeQ8q6Kg,161.84,10,90,0,0,Float](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fmap.naver.com%2Fp%3Fc%3D15.00%2C0%2C0%2C0%2Cadh%26p%3DG8MCOE5tzwznzZPeQ8q6Kg%2C161.84%2C10%2C90%2C0%2C0%2CFloat) ### Comment > 언제부턴가 해당 확장프로그렘이 켜져있을때 > 네이버지도에서 로드뷰를 보려고하면 화면이 멈추고 스크린샷과같이 오류가 뜹니다. > 사이트를 새로고침하거나 해당 프로그램을 비활성화를 하면 정상작동됩니다. > > 처음에는 로드뷰를 쓰기 어려울만큼 버벅임과 로드뷰 화질저화등이 발생했는데 > 이 확장프로그램에서 몇가지 설정 변경 후 화질개선과 버벅거림은 없어졌지만 > 첫 실행시 오류가 뜨는건 바뀌지않아요. > 네이버지도 로드뷰 관련 프로그램을 차단하는거같은데 수정부탁드립니다. ### Screenshots
Screenshot 1 ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/nbbifrcvywgsks80s44cwo804sc0gcwcu7ttg67gbk.png?nc=1)
Screenshot 1 with AdGuard disabled ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/li7hxnx6q1skcck4s8kkk0wcwkg044wctxta2icodw.png?nc=1)
### System configuration | Information | Value | |-------------|-------| AdGuard product: | AdGuard Browser Extension v5.4.3.49 MV3 System version: | `Windows 11` Browser: | Chrome Filters: | **Ad Blocking:**
AdGuard Base filter,
AdGuard Mobile Ads filter

**Privacy:**
AdGuard Tracking Protection filter

**Other:**
Filter unblocking search ads and self-promotion

**Language-specific:**

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://music.bugs.co.kr/search](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fmusic.bugs.co.kr%2Fsearch) ### Comment > 음악 스트리밍 사이트 벅스 music.bugs.co.kr 에서 검색어를 입력했을시 해당 스크린샷 처럼 아무것도 뜨지 않는 현상이 간간히 발생합니다 예전에 추적 보호에서 ip주소 숨기기를 했을때는 어떤걸 검색해도 스크린샷 같이 아무것도 뜨지 않는 현상이 발생했었거든요 그래서 이 옵션은 해제를 하고 사용하고 있는데.. 검색어를 입력할때마다 이런 현상이 간간히 자주 발생하고 있어서 이번에도 애드 가드 문제가 아닐까 생각중입니다 어떤 옵션 때문인지 이런 현상이 생기는지는 잘 모르겠습니다 간간히 발생하는것이라 다시 검색하면 제대로 뜰때가 많아서 옵션을 하나 하나 껏다 켰다 해서 이 옵션이 문제인지 아닌지 확실하게 알수가 없습니다 5월달부터 해당 문제가 발생한것 같고 그렇게 오래 되지는 않았습니다 추적 보호나 네트워크 관련 문제인듯 한데 도저히 어떤것인지 알수 없어서 답답합니다 어떤것 때문인지 확실하게 말해 드릴수 없어서 정말 죄송합니다 ### Screenshots

comment.body: As I understand, you have used AdGuard for Android and it doesn't relate to filters or AdGuard DNS. In that case, try to disable `Filter HTTPS traffic` in the `App managment` for the AppGallery, clear its cache, restart it.

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://map.naver.com/p?c=15.00,0,0,0,adh&p=G8MCOE5tzwznzZPeQ8q6Kg,161.84,10,90,0,0,Float](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fmap.naver.com%2Fp%3Fc%3D15.00%2C0%2C0%2C0%2Cadh%26p%3DG8MCOE5tzwznzZPeQ8q6Kg%2C161.84%2C10%2C90%2C0%2C0%2CFloat) ### Comment > 언제부턴가 해당 확장프로그렘이 켜져있을때 > 네이버지도에서 로드뷰를 보려고하면 화면이 멈추고 스크린샷과같이 오류가 뜹니다. > 사이트를 새로고침하거나 해당 프로그램을 비활성화를 하면 정상작동됩니다. > > 처음에는 로드뷰를 쓰기 어려울만큼 버벅임과 로드뷰 화질저화등이 발생했는데 > 이 확장프로그램에서 몇가지 설정 변경 후 화질개선과 버벅거림은 없어졌지만 > 첫 실행시 오류가 뜨는건 바뀌지않아요. > 네이버지도 로드뷰 관련 프로그램을 차단하는거같은데 수정부탁드립니다. ### Screenshots

labels.name: T: Incorrect Blocking
labels.description: Website or app not working properly due to filtering

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking)
[https://www.wunderground.com/dashboard/pws/IKROUN1](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.wunderground.com%2Fdashboard%2Fpws%2FIKROUN1)

### Comment
> Hello,
> 
> I would like to report a possible false positive or filtering issue with AdGuard DNS (dns.adguard-dns.com).
> 
> When AdGuard DNS is enabled on Android devices, Personal Weather Station (PWS) pages on Weather Underground do not load correctly.
> 
> What I tested:
> 
> Android phones with AdGuard DNS configured as Private DNS (dns.adguard-dns.com)
> Multiple browsers (Chrome and Opera)
> Multiple devices (the issue occurs on more than one Android phone)
> Clearing browser cache and site data did not resolve the issue
> 
> Observed behavior:
> 
> With AdGuard DNS enabled, station pages may either:
> display the station as Offline with no weather data shown, or
> redirect to a 404 Not Found page.
> 
> With AdGuard DNS disabled, the same station pages load correctly and weather data is displayed.
> With AdGuard DNS enabled but traffic routed through a VPN, the same station pages also load correctly.
> 
> The issue appears consistently on multiple devices using AdGuard DNS and disappears immediately when AdGuard DNS is disabled or bypassed through a VPN.
> 
> This suggests that a domain required by Weather Underground may be blocked or filtered by AdGuard DNS.
> 
> Thank you for investigating.



### Screenshots
<details>
  <summary>Screenshot 1</summary>

  ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/d904cbqzqc084oc088sg8kok4cco8w0ivt2646w9kg.jpg?nc=1)
</details>

<details>
  <summary>Screenshot 1 with AdGuard disabled</summary>

  ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/8aan3xf8e6ck8oc8o8ssoc8s0oo0cgooiqtwpclkfk.jpg?nc=1)
</details>

### Issue URL (Ads) [https://taggrs.io/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Ftaggrs.io%2F) ### Comment > Taggrs.io is a server-side tagging platform. > > The tracking loader is deployed on first-party domains and uses randomized path names, making domain-based blocking impossible. > > Observed request pattern: > https://subdomain.example.com/randomname.js?tg=M3JC64F > > > Examples from multiple websites: > > https://happytagging.taggrs.io/mzdszqmamq.js?tg=M3JC64F > > https://sst.flitsmeister.com/nxwbdbh9jy.js?tg=5HX8DSNQ > > https://sst.onlineveilingmeester.nl/7U0BH03X6F.js?tg=K783ZWS > > https://sst.webwinkelvakdagen.nl/CXSm0RrrWG.js?tg=WLT7DH > > The script initializes analytics/tracking functionality and subsequently sends tracking requests to first-party endpoints. > > Suggested EasyPrivacy filter: > > .js?tg=[A-Z0-9]{4,8}$/$script > > The tg parameter appears to be specific to the Taggrs loader. The path name varies between deployments, which is why a regular expression is proposed instead of a URL-specific filter. ### Screenshots
Screenshot 1 ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/66fp48dn0ikgkkck88wcwg4c80o48skdqtt3e9cbsg.png?nc=1)

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Ads) [https://24center.fi/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2F24center.fi%2F) ### Comment > Tracklution first-party tracking endpoints not covered by current privacy lists > > Tracklution is a server-side tracking and attribution platform that deploys tracking endpoints on first-party subdomains. > > Observed pattern: > > * Tracking script loaded from tralut.<site-domain> > * Tracking data sent to /collect/value > > Examples: > > 24center.fi > > Script: > > https://tralut.24center.fi/js/tracking.js?version=2023-11-21 > > Tracking endpoint: > > https://tralut.24center.fi/collect/value?vs=80e2c11557b9fa7cf754a3373d6459ffbc00e6de&ga4clid=2109210071.1780669264|1780669263 > > bang.fi > > Script: > > https://tralut.bang.fi/js/script-dynamic.js?version=1734433540492 > > Tracking endpoint: > > https://tralut.bang.fi/collect/value?vs=88752ad6558862b45b882f149a57607e697fa6c5&ga4clid=682795340.1780668807|1780668813 > > tonefestguitargallery.com > > Script: > > https://tralut.tonefestguitargallery.com/js/script-dynamic.js?version=1761306466436 > > Tracking endpoint: > > https://tralut.tonefestguitargallery.com/collect/value?vs=e6fe7354dde738938abcc23107358e75ee02c39d&ga4clid=1184792895.1780668935|1780668935 > > ortho-clear.com > > Script: > > https://tralut.ortho-clear.com/js/script-dynamic.js?version=1741614506484 > > Tracking endpoint: > > https://tralut.ortho-clear.com/collect/value?vs=329e7ba2fa181bf2401d070a81c3106fcf4ce002&ga4clid=2085585196.1780668962|1780668962 > > --- > > Observed URL patterns: > > https://tralut.<domain>/js/tracking.js > > https://tralut.<domain>/js/script-dynamic.js > > https://tralut.<domain>/collect/value?vs= > > --- > > Additional observations: > > * The script stores identifiers in first-party context. > * Requests to /collect/value contain tracking identifiers such as vs and ga4clid. > * In my testing, requests continued to be sent after rejecting consent through the site’s CMP. > * The implementation appears to be designed specifically to operate through first-party subdomains. ### Screenshots

issue.title: 24center.fi

labels.name: T: Ads
labels.description: Missed ads

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Ads) [https://24center.fi/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2F24center.fi%2F) ### Comment > Tracklution first-party tracking endpoints not covered by current privacy lists > > Tracklution is a server-side tracking and attribution platform that deploys tracking endpoints on first-party subdomains. > > Observed pattern: > > * Tracking script loaded from tralut.<site-domain> > * Tracking data sent to /collect/value > > Examples: > > 24center.fi > > Script: > > https://tralut.24center.fi/js/tracking.js?version=2023-11-21 > > Tracking endpoint: > > https://tralut.24center.fi/collect/value?vs=80e2c11557b9fa7cf754a3373d6459ffbc00e6de&ga4clid=2109210071.1780669264|1780669263 > > bang.fi > > Script: > > https://tralut.bang.fi/js/script-dynamic.js?version=1734433540492 > > Tracking endpoint: > > https://tralut.bang.fi/collect/value?vs=88752ad6558862b45b882f149a57607e697fa6c5&ga4clid=682795340.1780668807|1780668813 > > tonefestguitargallery.com > > Script: > > https://tralut.tonefestguitargallery.com/js/script-dynamic.js?version=1761306466436 > > Tracking endpoint: > > https://tralut.tonefestguitargallery.com/collect/value?vs=e6fe7354dde738938abcc23107358e75ee02c39d&ga4clid=1184792895.1780668935|1780668935 > > ortho-clear.com > > Script: > > https://tralut.ortho-clear.com/js/script-dynamic.js?version=1741614506484 > > Tracking endpoint: > > https://tralut.ortho-clear.com/collect/value?vs=329e7ba2fa181bf2401d070a81c3106fcf4ce002&ga4clid=2085585196.1780668962|1780668962 > > --- > > Observed URL patterns: > > https://tralut.<domain>/js/tracking.js > > https://tralut.<domain>/js/script-dynamic.js > > https://tralut.<domain>/collect/value?vs= > > --- > > Additional observations: > > * The script stores identifiers in first-party context. > * Requests to /collect/value contain tracking identifiers such as vs and ga4clid. > * In my testing, requests continued to be sent after rejecting consent through the site’s CMP. > * The implementation appears to be designed specifically to operate through first-party subdomains. ### Screenshots

This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 7 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://music.bugs.co.kr/search](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fmusic.bugs.co.kr%2Fsearch) ### Comment > 음악 스트리밍 사이트 벅스 music.bugs.co.kr 에서 검색어를 입력했을시 해당 스크린샷 처럼 아무것도 뜨지 않는 현상이 간간히 발생합니다 예전에 추적 보호에서 ip주소 숨기기를 했을때는 어떤걸 검색해도 스크린샷 같이 아무것도 뜨지 않는 현상이 발생했었거든요 그래서 이 옵션은 해제를 하고 사용하고 있는데.. 검색어를 입력할때마다 이런 현상이 간간히 자주 발생하고 있어서 이번에도 애드 가드 문제가 아닐까 생각중입니다 어떤 옵션 때문인지 이런 현상이 생기는지는 잘 모르겠습니다 간간히 발생하는것이라 다시 검색하면 제대로 뜰때가 많아서 옵션을 하나 하나 껏다 켰다 해서 이 옵션이 문제인지 아닌지 확실하게 알수가 없습니다 5월달부터 해당 문제가 발생한것 같고 그렇게 오래 되지는 않았습니다 추적 보호나 네트워크 관련 문제인듯 한데 도저히 어떤것인지 알수 없어서 답답합니다 어떤것 때문인지 확실하게 말해 드릴수 없어서 정말 죄송합니다 ### Screenshots

- [x] This site DOES NOT contain sexually explicit material, otherwise use [NSFW-specific form](./new?template=bug_report_NSFW.yml);

- [x] AdGuard product version is up-to-date;
- [x] Browser version is up-to-date;
- [x] If the site or app is broken, disabling AdGuard protection resolves an issue.

### What product do you use?

Other ad blocker

### AdGuard version

uBlock Origin v1.71.0

### What type of problem have you encountered?

Website or app doesn't work properly

### Which browser(s) do you use?

other (specify in comment below)

### Which device do you use?

Desktop

### Where is the problem encountered?

https://www.ringo.no/

### Ad Blocking

AdGuard Base filter

### Privacy

AdGuard Tracking Protection filter

### Social Widgets

_No response_

### Annoyances

_No response_

### Security

_No response_

### Other

_No response_

### DNS filters

_No response_

### Language-specific

_No response_

### What Stealth Mode options do you have enabled?

_No response_

### Add your comment and screenshots

### Issue:
Clicking on search results have no effect.

### How to reproduce:
**1:** Use the site's search function to search for something (e.g. "Star Wars").
(The search results appears in a popup window)

**2:** Click on one of the search results..
* **Expected result:** The product page for the item that you clicked on should open.
* **Actual result:** Nothing happens!

### Problem filter:
* **AdGuard Tracking Protection filter**

### Problem rule:
`||api.clerk.io/v*/log/`

### Possible fix:
`@@||api.clerk.io/v*/log/click?payload=$script,domain=ringo.no`

--

### System info:
* OS/version: Windows 10 Pro 22H2 (x64) [ESU]
* Browser/version: LibreWolf v151.0.3-1 (x64) [Tracking Protection = Standard]
* Adblock Extension/version: uBlock Origin v1.71.0

### Privacy

- [x] I agree to follow this condition

issue.body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] This site DOES NOT contain sexually explicit material, otherwise use [NSFW-specific form](./new?template=bug_report_NSFW.yml);
- [x] Filters were updated before reproducing an issue;
- [x] AdGuard product version is up-to-date;
- [x] Browser version is up-to-date;
- [x] If the site or app is broken, disabling AdGuard protection resolves an issue.

### What product do you use?

Other ad blocker

### AdGuard version

uBlock Origin v1.71.0

### What type of problem have you encountered?

Website or app doesn't work properly

### Which browser(s) do you use?

other (specify in comment below)

### Which device do you use?

Desktop

### Where is the problem encountered?

https://www.ringo.no/

### Ad Blocking

AdGuard Base filter

### Privacy

AdGuard Tracking Protection filter

### Social Widgets

_No response_

### Annoyances

_No response_

### Security

_No response_

### Other

_No response_

### DNS filters

_No response_

### Language-specific

_No response_

### What Stealth Mode options do you have enabled?

_No response_

### Add your comment and screenshots

### Issue:
Clicking on search results have no effect.

### How to reproduce:
**1:** Use the site's search function to search for something (e.g. "Star Wars").
(The search results appears in a popup window)

**2:** Click on one of the search results..
* **Expected result:** The product page for the item that you clicked on should open.
* **Actual result:** Nothing happens!

### Problem filter:
* **AdGuard Tracking Protection filter**

### Problem rule:
`||api.clerk.io/v*/log/`

### Possible fix:
`@@||api.clerk.io/v*/log/click?payload=$script,domain=ringo.no`

--

### System info:
* OS/version: Windows 10 Pro 22H2 (x64) [ESU]
* Browser/version: LibreWolf v151.0.3-1 (x64) [Tracking Protection = Standard]
* Adblock Extension/version: uBlock Origin v1.71.0

### Privacy

- [x] I agree to follow this condition

issue.title: taggrs.io

labels.name: T: Ads
labels.description: Missed ads

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Ads) [https://taggrs.io/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Ftaggrs.io%2F) ### Comment > Taggrs.io is a server-side tagging platform. > > The tracking loader is deployed on first-party domains and uses randomized path names, making domain-based blocking impossible. > > Observed request pattern: > https://subdomain.example.com/randomname.js?tg=M3JC64F > > > Examples from multiple websites: > > https://happytagging.taggrs.io/mzdszqmamq.js?tg=M3JC64F > > https://sst.flitsmeister.com/nxwbdbh9jy.js?tg=5HX8DSNQ > > https://sst.onlineveilingmeester.nl/7U0BH03X6F.js?tg=K783ZWS > > https://sst.webwinkelvakdagen.nl/CXSm0RrrWG.js?tg=WLT7DH > > The script initializes analytics/tracking functionality and subsequently sends tracking requests to first-party endpoints. > > Suggested EasyPrivacy filter: > > .js?tg=[A-Z0-9]{4,8}$/$script > > The tg parameter appears to be specific to the Taggrs loader. The path name varies between deployments, which is why a regular expression is proposed instead of a URL-specific filter. ### Screenshots
Screenshot 1 ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/66fp48dn0ikgkkck88wcwg4c80o48skdqtt3e9cbsg.png?nc=1)

issue.title: bilibili.com - History API tracking circumvention for logged-in users

labels.name: A: In progress
labels.description: Work on the issue is in progress

labels.name: A: Waiting for data
labels.description: Waiting for more information from the user

issue.body:
## Problem

Bilibili.com re-injects stripped tracking parameters back into URLs via the History API for logged-in users. After the `$removeparam` rules (already in TrackParamFilter) strip `vd_source`, `trackid`, and `spm_id_from` from URLs on page load, Bilibili's JavaScript uses `history.pushState` and `history.replaceState` to add these parameters back, bypassing URL-level filtering.

## Affected parameters

- `vd_source`
- `trackid`
- `spm_id_from`

## Proposed fix

Use `trusted-replace-argument` scriptlets to intercept `history.pushState` and `history.replaceState` calls and strip these three parameters from the URL argument before the call executes.

```
bilibili.com#%#//scriptlet('trusted-replace-argument', 'history.pushState', '2', 'repl:/([?&])vd_source=[^&#]*&|[?&]vd_source=[^&#]*(?=#|$)/$1/')
bilibili.com#%#//scriptlet('trusted-replace-argument', 'history.replaceState', '2', 'repl:/([?&])vd_source=[^&#]*&|[?&]vd_source=[^&#]*(?=#|$)/$1/')
bilibili.com#%#//scriptlet('trusted-replace-argument', 'history.pushState', '2', 'repl:/([?&])trackid=[^&#]*&|[?&]trackid=[^&#]*(?=#|$)/$1/')
bilibili.com#%#//scriptlet('trusted-replace-argument', 'history.replaceState', '2', 'repl:/([?&])trackid=[^&#]*&|[?&]trackid=[^&#]*(?=#|$)/$1/')
bilibili.com#%#//scriptlet('trusted-replace-argument', 'history.pushState', '2', 'repl:/([?&])spm_id_from=[^&#]*&|[?&]spm_id_from=[^&#]*(?=#|$)/$1/')
bilibili.com#%#//scriptlet('trusted-replace-argument', 'history.replaceState', '2', 'repl:/([?&])spm_id_from=[^&#]*&|[?&]spm_id_from=[^&#]*(?=#|$)/$1/')
```

## Steps to reproduce

1. Log in to bilibili.com
2. Navigate to any video page with tracking parameters in the URL (e.g., `?vd_source=xxx`)
3. Observe that after page load, the tracking parameters are re-added to the URL via History API

## Environment

- AdGuard Browser Extension / AdGuard for Windows/Mac/Android
- Logged-in bilibili.com user

comment.body:
It's not incorrect syntax, it's treated as constant value string. It replaces the third parameter with the value passed as an argument.
Screenshots First original argument from website: Now replaced by value in scriptlet:
So, for example on `https://search.bilibili.com/all?vt=39157436&keyword=cs`, the passed value by website is: ``` https://search.bilibili.com/all?vt=39157436&keyword=cs ``` and scriptlet replaces it with: ``` repl:/(?<=[?&])(?:vd_source|trackid|spm_id_from)=[^&#]*&?//g ``` But the problem is that it causes that `SecurityError` is thrown: ``` SecurityError: Failed to execute 'replaceState' on 'History': A history state object with URL 'repl:/(?%3C=[?&])(?:vd_source|trackid|spm_id_from)=[^&#]*&?//g' cannot be created in a document with origin 'https://search.bilibili.com' and URL 'https://search.bilibili.com/all?vt=39157436&keyword=cs'. ``` and in this case when error is thrown, it's catched by website and then website is "reloaded".
Code: ```js function o(l, u, c) { const d = e.indexOf("#") , f = d > -1 ? (n.host && document.querySelector("base") ? e : e.slice(d)) + l : YU() + e + l; try { t[c ? "replaceState" : "pushState"](u, "", f), a.value = u } catch (p) { console.error(p), n[c ? "replace" : "assign"](f) } } ```

issue.title: bilibili.com - History API tracking circumvention for logged-in users

labels.name: A: In progress
labels.description: Work on the issue is in progress

labels.name: A: Waiting for data
labels.description: Waiting for more information from the user

issue.body:
## Problem

Bilibili.com re-injects stripped tracking parameters back into URLs via the History API for logged-in users. After the `$removeparam` rules (already in TrackParamFilter) strip `vd_source`, `trackid`, and `spm_id_from` from URLs on page load, Bilibili's JavaScript uses `history.pushState` and `history.replaceState` to add these parameters back, bypassing URL-level filtering.

## Affected parameters

- `vd_source`
- `trackid`
- `spm_id_from`

## Proposed fix

Use `trusted-replace-argument` scriptlets to intercept `history.pushState` and `history.replaceState` calls and strip these three parameters from the URL argument before the call executes.

```
bilibili.com#%#//scriptlet('trusted-replace-argument', 'history.pushState', '2', 'repl:/([?&])vd_source=[^&#]*&|[?&]vd_source=[^&#]*(?=#|$)/$1/')
bilibili.com#%#//scriptlet('trusted-replace-argument', 'history.replaceState', '2', 'repl:/([?&])vd_source=[^&#]*&|[?&]vd_source=[^&#]*(?=#|$)/$1/')
bilibili.com#%#//scriptlet('trusted-replace-argument', 'history.pushState', '2', 'repl:/([?&])trackid=[^&#]*&|[?&]trackid=[^&#]*(?=#|$)/$1/')
bilibili.com#%#//scriptlet('trusted-replace-argument', 'history.replaceState', '2', 'repl:/([?&])trackid=[^&#]*&|[?&]trackid=[^&#]*(?=#|$)/$1/')
bilibili.com#%#//scriptlet('trusted-replace-argument', 'history.pushState', '2', 'repl:/([?&])spm_id_from=[^&#]*&|[?&]spm_id_from=[^&#]*(?=#|$)/$1/')
bilibili.com#%#//scriptlet('trusted-replace-argument', 'history.replaceState', '2', 'repl:/([?&])spm_id_from=[^&#]*&|[?&]spm_id_from=[^&#]*(?=#|$)/$1/')
```

## Steps to reproduce

1. Log in to bilibili.com
2. Navigate to any video page with tracking parameters in the URL (e.g., `?vd_source=xxx`)
3. Observe that after page load, the tracking parameters are re-added to the URL via History API

## Environment

- AdGuard Browser Extension / AdGuard for Windows/Mac/Android
- Logged-in bilibili.com user

comment.body:
Weird. Now I see these problems. Changed the rules like you suggested and enabled them.
But why did rules with incorrect syntax work for me before? And if the argument is incorrect, why did the scriptlet run at all?
